title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that enables calling components as asynchronous functions. It eliminates boilerplate by handling modals through a stack-based architecture with concepts like Upsert Singletons and Mutation Flow, replacing the need for React Portals and context provider patterns.
writeup: This video from Better Stack covers react-call, a lightweight React utility that lets components be invoked as asynchronous functions. It matters because modal handling in React often relies on Portals and context provider patterns that add boilerplate, and react-call replaces that approach with a stack-based architecture. The concrete takeaways: react-call manages modals through concepts like Upsert Singletons and Mutation Flow, removing the need for React Portals and context management for these cases.
